newModal.applicabilityInfo: Mark Not Applicable when scope legitimately excludes this control (e.g. no payment processing for PCI). The justification is required and appears verbatim in the SoA report.

roi.across: Across {quantified} of {linked, plural, one {# linked risk} other {# linked risks}} with a quantified ALE, priced at {effectiveness}% declared effectiveness.
roi.gapNote: ROI is null on purpose — the model never invents a number from missing inputs.

editModal.title: Edit Control

bulkImport.title: Bulk import evidence
bulkImport.description: Upload a ZIP of evidence files to extract into individual evidence records.
bulkImport.headerDescription: Drop a .zip and the worker will extract each supported file into its own evidence record.
bulkImport.dropHint: Up to 100 MB per archive. PDF / Office / image / CSV / JSON entries are extracted; other entries are skipped.

textModal.title: Add evidence
textModal.description: Record a link, narrative, or attestation against a control.

upload.title: Upload evidence
upload.description: Drag and drop one or more files — PDF, Office, CSV, image, JSON, or ZIP. Each file becomes its own evidence record.
upload.headerDescription: Drag and drop one or more files (or click to browse). Files share the metadata you set below.
upload.dropHint: PDF, Office, CSV, image, JSON, or ZIP — up to {mb} MB per file
upload.titlePlaceholderMulti: Each file uses its own filename
upload.titleMultiHint: Per-file titles default to the filename when uploading multiple files.
upload.retainInfo: After this date the evidence is archived out of the active set. It stays in the audit log — admins can still recover it.
upload.retainHint: Optional — applies to every file in the batch.
upload.controlsEmptyHint: No controls available to link yet.
upload.folderFieldDescription: Optional — group this upload with related evidence.
upload.fileRequired: A file is required to upload evidence.

upload.controlSearchHintPlural: Search across {count} controls by annex id, code, or name.

admin.title: Administration
admin.templateDescription: Available policy templates to quick-start your compliance documentation.

apiKeys.fullAccessTooltip: Gives this key read + write on every resource — evidence, controls, admin settings. Prefer narrow scopes for automation.
apiKeys.keyCopied: API key copied — paste it into your tool now.
apiKeys.keyCopyFailed: Copy failed — select the key and copy manually.
apiKeys.copyKeyNow: Copy this key now — it will never be shown again!
apiKeys.createdSuccess: API key "{name}" created. Copy the key below.
apiKeys.revokedCount: Revoked {count, plural, one {# API key} other {# API keys}}.
apiKeys.heading: API Keys
apiKeys.description: Manage machine-to-machine API keys for programmatic access. Keys are scoped to specific resources and actions.
apiKeys.expiryTooltip: Keys with no expiry stay valid until someone manually revokes them. Set a deadline for any automation or third-party integration.
apiKeys.confirmRevokeDesc: Key prefix {prefix}… will stop working immediately. Any integration using it will lose access. This cannot be undone.

billing.heading: Billing
billing.description: Manage your workspace plan and billing for {name}.
billing.trialDaysLeft: {count, plural, one {# day} other {# days}} left in your trial
billing.trialEndsOn: Trial ends on {date}. Upgrade to keep access to premium features.
billing.paymentIssueBody: Please update your payment method to avoid service interruption.
billing.selfHostedBody: This instance runs without a Stripe integration — plan limits resolve to ENTERPRISE for every tenant and the in-app upgrade / portal buttons are disabled. Subscription changes are managed by your operator.
billing.manageSubscriptionBody: Update payment method, view invoices, or change your plan via the Stripe Customer Portal.

entra.heading: Microsoft Entra ID
entra.step1Item1: Create an App Registration in the Entra portal; set the redirect URI to {icUrl}/api/auth/callback/microsoft-entra-id.
entra.step1Item2: In Token configuration, add a groups claim set to Security groups (manifest "groupMembershipClaims": "SecurityGroup"). IC cannot set this for you — without it, no group claims reach IC.
entra.enforceGateHint: When on, a user who matches none of your group → role mappings is denied access. Only takes effect once you've added at least one mapping below.
entra.saveFailedGuid: Save failed — check the directory + client IDs are valid GUIDs.

integrations.consentFlowError: Could not start the SharePoint consent flow.
integrations.spDescription: Source compliance evidence from SharePoint document libraries and keep policies in sync. Connecting grants IC delegated read access to the sites you choose.
integrations.chooseSites: Choose which sites IC may access:
integrations.heading: Integrations
integrations.noIntegrations: No integrations configured. Click "Add Integration" to get started.
integrations.secretsEncrypted: Secrets are encrypted at rest. They cannot be viewed after saving.

ledgerIntegrity.foundBreak: Reconciliation found a hash-chain break — review the report below.
ledgerIntegrity.noRuns: No reconciliation runs yet. Run one to verify the stock ledger.
ledgerIntegrity.neverRunSubtitle: Run a reconciliation to verify the append-only stock ledger's hash chain.
ledgerIntegrity.driftSubtitle: Hash-chain break at entry #{num}. Do not mutate the ledger — see the runbook. Last run {when}.
ledgerIntegrity.unknownSubtitle: Verdict unavailable for this run. Last run {when}.

modules.heading: Modules
modules.description: Turn modules off to hide the parts of the product your team doesn't use. A disabled module's pages and APIs return a permission error until you turn it back on. Leaving everything on is the default.
modules.everyModuleOff: Every module is off — only core admin and settings remain available.

notificationSettings.confirmRunJob: Are you sure you want to run the {job} job now?
notificationSettings.heading: Email Notifications
notificationSettings.bccNote: All outbound emails will be BCC'd to this address.

riskAppetite.heading: Risk Appetite
riskAppetite.simulatedNote: simulated portfolio percentile (falls back to Σ of means until a simulation runs)

roles.baseRoleHint: Used for coarse authorization when custom permissions don't apply.
roles.descriptionPlaceholder: Optional — describe what this role is for
roles.memberWarning: {count} member(s) will lose this custom role and fall back to their base role.
roles.heading: Custom Roles
roles.rolesDefined: {count, plural, one {# custom role} other {# custom roles}} defined. Members assigned a custom role use its permissions instead of the built-in role defaults.
roles.noRoles: No custom roles defined yet. Click "Create Custom Role" to get started.

scim.noPermission: You do not have permission to view SCIM tokens.
scim.tokenCopied: SCIM token copied — paste it into your IdP now.
scim.copyFailed: Copy failed — select the token and copy manually.
scim.heading: SCIM Provisioning
scim.description: Automate user provisioning from your identity provider (Okta, Azure AD, OneLogin).
scim.endpointHelp: Use this base URL when configuring SCIM in your identity provider.
scim.tokenNotShownAgain: This token will not be shown again. Store it securely in your identity provider.
scim.noActiveTokensHelp: Generate a token to enable automated provisioning from your identity provider.
scim.revokedTokenCount: {count, plural, one {# revoked token} other {# revoked tokens}}
scim.guide1: Generate a SCIM token above and copy it securely.
scim.guide2: In your IdP (Okta, Azure AD, etc.), configure a SCIM 2.0 provisioning connector.
scim.guide3: Set the SCIM connector base URL to the endpoint shown above.
scim.guide4: Set Authentication to "HTTP Header" with the bearer token.
scim.guide5: Enable provisioning actions: Create Users, Update User Attributes, Deactivate Users.
scim.guide6: Test the connection from your IdP's SCIM provisioning settings.
scim.roleMapping: Role mapping: SCIM-provisioned users are assigned the Reader role by default. Editors and Auditors can be mapped via your IdP's group/role assignment. Admin role cannot be assigned via SCIM — it must be set manually.
scim.confirmRevokeDesc: Any IdP using this token will lose access immediately. This cannot be undone.

security.confirmRevokeMine: This will sign you out of all devices. Continue?
security.mySessionsRevoked: Your sessions have been revoked. You will be signed out shortly.
security.confirmRevokeAll: WARNING: This will sign out ALL users in your organization. Are you sure?
security.allSessionsRevoked: Sessions revoked for {count} users. Everyone will need to sign in again.

DISABLED.label: Disabled
DISABLED.description: MFA is not available. Users cannot enroll in multi-factor authentication.

OPTIONAL.label: Optional
OPTIONAL.description: Users can choose to enable MFA. Enrolled users will be challenged at login.

REQUIRED.label: Required
REQUIRED.description: All users must enroll in MFA. Users without MFA will be redirected to enrollment on login.

security.heading: Security & MFA
security.mfaPolicyTooltip: Applies tenant-wide on the next login. Switching to Required forces everyone who isn't enrolled into the enrolment flow before they can access any page.
security.mfaPolicyDesc: Configure whether MFA is required, optional, or disabled for your organization.
security.beforeRequired1: Ensure you (the admin) have enrolled in MFA first
security.beforeRequired2: Users without MFA will be redirected to enrollment on their next login
security.beforeRequired3: Break-glass admin access is preserved via SSO if configured
security.sessionSettingsDesc: Configure session timeout for your organization. Leave blank for the default.
security.maxSessionAgeTooltip: Absolute lifetime of a login session — users must re-authenticate after this many minutes regardless of activity. Leave blank to inherit the platform default.
security.sessionManagementDesc: Revoke active sessions. Revoked users must sign in again.
security.signOutOthersDesc: Invalidate all your active sessions across devices.
security.revokeAllUsersDesc: Force all organization members to sign in again. Use for incidents.
security.revokeSpecificUser: Revoke sessions for a specific user

sso.heading: SSO & Identity
sso.description: Configure enterprise single sign-on for your workspace.
sso.nameIdFormatTooltip: Leave empty if your IdP issues email-format NameIDs (the common case). Override only if your IdP uses a non-standard URN — check the IdP's SAML attribute mapping.
sso.enforceTooltip: Everyone must sign in via your IdP once this saves. Local-password admins keep break-glass access — confirm at least one admin has a working password before enabling.
sso.enforcedNotice: When enforced, users must authenticate via SSO. Only admins with a local password can bypass (break-glass access).
sso.howItWorks1: • Users must already have an account and tenant membership to login via SSO
sso.howItWorks2: • SSO links are created automatically on first successful login
sso.howItWorks3: • When enforced, only admins with a local password can bypass SSO (break-glass)
sso.howItWorks4: • Email domains help auto-discover SSO on the login page
sso.confirmDeleteDesc: Users will need to use local login until SSO is reconfigured. This cannot be undone.

vendorTemplates.heading: Vendor questionnaire templates
vendorTemplates.description: Author and publish reusable assessment templates. Edits on a published template require cloning to a new draft revision.
vendorTemplates.noTemplatesDesc: Use the form above to create your first vendor questionnaire template.

vendorTemplateBuilder.publishedBannerBody: Edits to a published template would invalidate any live assessments pinned to its version. Clone it to a new draft revision to continue editing.
vendorTemplateBuilder.noQuestions: No questions yet — drag here or add one below.
vendorTemplateBuilder.promptPlaceholder: Do you encrypt data at rest?

vendorReview.awaitingRespondentBody: The vendor hasn't submitted yet. Review controls unlock once the assessment moves to SUBMITTED.

members.inviteCreatedNoEmail: Invite created for {email}, but the email could not be sent (check SMTP config). Share this link: {link}
members.confirmDeactivate: Deactivate {email}? They will lose access to this tenant.
members.confirmRevokeSession: Revoke this session? The user will be signed out from this device on their next request.
members.heading: Members & Roles
members.sessionsModalDesc: Live sessions for this member. Revoke any device to sign it out on its next request.
members.activeSessionsCount: {count, plural, one {# active session} other {# active sessions}}.
members.noActiveSessionsDesc: This user is not currently signed in on any device.
members.certificatesDesc: Plant-protection certificates carried by this member — printed on the БАБХ ДНЕВНИК.
members.certificatesEditableNote: Editable per member. Blank clears a field.

riskManager.noTemplates: No templates available. Try creating from scratch.

riskManager.csvDesc: Upload a CSV file with at minimum a title column. Optional columns: description, category, likelihood (1-5), impact (1-5), owner.
riskManager.noValidRows: No valid rows found. Ensure CSV has a "title" column header.
riskManager.noImportPermission: You don't have permission to import risks.
riskManager.noControlsAvailable: No controls available. Add controls first from the Controls page.
riskManager.aleHistogramDesc: Each quantified risk lands in a log-scale loss bucket, stacked by its matrix band — the distribution the heatmap structurally compresses.
riskManager.noQuantifiedRisks: No quantified risks yet — set FAIR ranges or SLE × ARO on a risk to populate the histogram.
riskManager.cellCollisionsDesc: Risks sharing a matrix cell whose loss estimates differ more than 10× — the matrix cannot show the difference.
riskManager.noRisksMatchDesc: Try widening your search or clearing one of the active filters.
riskManager.noRisksDesc: Identify a threat, score its likelihood × impact, and link the controls that mitigate it.

automationSuggestions.intro: Automations that would close gaps in your current posture — each scored, with a rationale. Create one as a draft, then refine it.
automationSuggestions.noSuggestions: No new suggestions — your enabled rules already cover the obvious gaps.

onboarding.bannerDesc: Finish the onboarding wizard to configure your compliance workspace.
onboarding.accessRestrictedDesc: Only tenant administrators can access the onboarding wizard.
onboarding.workspaceReady: Your workspace is ready. Redirecting to dashboard...
onboarding.welcome: Welcome! Let's set up your workspace.
onboarding.welcomeDesc: This wizard will guide you through configuring your compliance platform in just a few steps.
onboarding.onboardingCompleteDesc: Your workspace has been configured. You can always update settings from the admin panel.
onboarding.setupWizardDesc: Complete these steps to configure your compliance workspace.
onboarding.companyProfileIntro: Tell us about your organization. This information helps tailor your compliance experience.
onboarding.frameworkIntro: Select the compliance frameworks you want to implement. You can add more later.
onboarding.fwIsoDesc: International information security management standard. Installs 93 controls across 4 domains.
onboarding.fwNis2Desc: EU cybersecurity directive for essential and important entities. Installs sector-specific requirements.
onboarding.selectAtLeastOne: Select at least one framework to continue.
onboarding.assetIntro: Add your key information assets. These are the systems, databases, and services you need to protect.
onboarding.assetHint: You can import assets in bulk later from the Assets page.
onboarding.controlIntro: We'll install baseline controls from your selected frameworks. This creates your initial control register.
onboarding.noFrameworksSelected: No frameworks selected. Go back to the Frameworks step to select at least one.
onboarding.riskIntro: Generate a starter risk register based on your assets and selected frameworks.
onboarding.starterRiskDesc: We'll generate common information security risks based on industry best practices. You can customize, add, or remove risks later.
onboarding.teamIntro: Invite your team members. They'll receive an email invitation to join your workspace.
onboarding.teamHint: You can skip this step and invite team members later from the Admin panel.
onboarding.reviewIntro: Review your setup before completing onboarding.
onboarding.reviewNotice: Click "Complete Setup" below to finish onboarding and go to your dashboard.

installPrompt.installDesc: Add it to your home screen — works in the field, online or off.
installPrompt.a2hsAria: Add AgriSaaS to your home screen
installPrompt.a2hsDesc: Tap the Share icon, then “Add to Home Screen” to install AgriSaaS for offline field use.

newRisk.title: New risk
newRisk.description: Capture a risk against your register.
newRisk.headerDescription: Capture a risk and optionally pre-fill from a template.
newRisk.templateDescription: Pre-fills title, category, likelihood, and impact from the selected template.

riskEvaluation.title: Risk Evaluation
riskEvaluation.likelihoodHelp: Inherent probability of this scenario in the next 12 months, ignoring current controls. 1 = rare, 5 = almost certain.
riskEvaluation.impactHelp: Severity to the organisation if the risk materialises. 1 = minor / isolated, 5 = catastrophic / regulatory.

riskDetail.aleAria: Annualised loss expectancy {label} — open the quantification tab

riskAssessment.kriNudge: A leading signal has moved since this risk was last assessed — re-assess the likelihood and impact below to bring the conclusion in line with the indicator.
riskAssessment.noEffectivenessSignal: no effectiveness signal (run a test or declare effectiveness)
riskAssessment.toDeriveResidual: to derive a residual from your control stack.
riskAssessment.legacyFormula: Set before decomposition — dimensions unknown (legacy formula).
riskAssessment.noDerivableResidual: No derivable residual — link controls with an effectiveness signal.
riskAssessment.residualBaselineWarning: Inherent has changed since you opened the residual draft — your residual now applies to L={likelihood} × I={impact}. Re-check the dimensions before saving.
riskAssessment.justificationPlaceholder: Why this residual differs from the derived suggestion
riskAssessment.bridgeQuantified: This risk already carries a FAIR loss estimate. Review or refine the analysis.
riskAssessment.bridgeUnquantified: Need loss numbers instead of bands? Run the FAIR analysis on this risk.

riskFair.title: FAIR Analysis
riskFair.intro: Calibrate each factor as a range — give the interval you're 90% sure contains the true value. The simulation samples the full range; the derived point is the Beta-PERT mean.

riskBowtie.title: Bow-Tie Analysis
riskBowtie.intro: Threats (left) → preventive barriers → the risk event → mitigating barriers → consequences (right). Barrier colour = control effectiveness.

riskHistory.empty: No history yet. Daily snapshots accrue once the risk-snapshot job has run.
riskHistory.title: History

riskAi.title: AI-Assisted Risk Assessment
riskAi.subtitle: Generate and review AI-suggested risks for your organization
riskAi.noAssetsFound: No assets found. Suggestions will be general.
riskAi.contextPlaceholder: Describe your industry, business model, regulatory environment, or specific concerns...
riskAi.analyzingDesc: The AI is generating risk suggestions based on your assets, frameworks, and context.
riskAi.mayTakeSeconds: This may take a few seconds
riskAi.creatingRecordsDesc: Adding accepted suggestions to your Risk Register.
riskAi.risksAdded: {count, plural, one {# risk added to your register} other {# risks added to your register}}
riskAi.appliedDesc: AI suggestions have been applied. You can review and refine them in the Risk Register.
riskAi.selectAtLeastOne: Select at least one suggestion to accept

riskCorrelations.title: Risk Correlation Matrix
riskCorrelations.clickCell: Click a cell in the upper triangle to edit a pair.

riskHierarchy.title: Risk Hierarchy
riskHierarchy.noNodes: No nodes yet. Add a node, then link risks to it (via the hierarchy API or the risk form).

riskKri.title: Key Risk Indicators
riskKri.name: Name
riskKri.noKris: No KRIs yet. Create one to track a leading indicator with RAG thresholds.

riskLossEvents.title: Loss events
riskLossEvents.intro: The system predicts losses everywhere — FAIR ALE, the simulated portfolio P50/P90, the loss-exceedance curve. This is where the world's answer comes back, so the forecasts can be scored.
riskLossEvents.noActuals: No actuals recorded yet — the forecasting stack is unfalsifiable until losses come back in. Record the next one below, or enable the finding / incident close-out integration to record them automatically.
riskLossEvents.whatHappenedPlaceholder: Customer breach response — settlement + vendor X

riskScenarios.title: Risk Scenarios
riskScenarios.newScenarioDesc: Create a what-if scenario, then simulate to compare its portfolio VaR against the live baseline. Overrides (field patches / synthetic risks) are authored via the scenarios API.
riskScenarios.name: Name

riskDashboard.noSimulation: No simulation yet — run one to compute the portfolio loss distribution, VaR, and the loss exceedance curve.
riskDashboard.lecDesc: For each annual-loss threshold (x), the simulated probability the year's total losses are ≥ that threshold.
riskDashboard.portfolioAppetiteNote: ≈{pct}% chance the year's losses exceed the {amount} portfolio appetite.
riskDashboard.perRiskAppetiteNote: {over} of {total} simulated risks carry a P90 loss above the {amount} per-risk appetite cap.

criticalRisks.title: Critical Risks
criticalRisks.summary: {count, plural, one {# critical risk (score ≥ 15, not closed) across the portfolio} other {# critical risks (score ≥ 15, not closed) across the portfolio}}
criticalRisks.emptyDesc: No risk with inherent score ≥ 15 is currently open across this organization's tenants.

calendar.description: Track every compliance deadline — evidence reviews, policy renewals, vendor renewals, audit cycles, control tests — in one place.

farmTasks.title: Farm Tasks
farmTasks.description: Your field-work queue — assigned farm tasks and operations, soonest-due first.
farmTasks.emptyDescription: Create a farm task to plan field work — tillage, planting, scouting, irrigation, harvest — and assign it to an operator. Tasks assigned to you appear here, soonest-due first.

farmTasks.titlePlaceholder: e.g. Scout the north block for aphids

journal.description: The durable record of work done — and planned — on the farm.

journal.emptyDescription: Log field activities, observations, input applications, and harvests — the field record behind every traceability claim.

photos.attachHint: Attach field photos and documents to this entry.
photos.emptyDescription: Upload a photo to start the visual record.
photos.emptyDescriptionReadonly: No photos have been attached to this entry.

detail.quantitiesEmptyDescription: This entry records no measured amount (an input application or harvest would carry one).

detail.chooseFileError: Choose a file to upload, or enter an evidence URL.

links.noLinksDescription: Cross-link this task to related tasks, controls, evidence, or risks via + Link.

accessReviews.title: Access Reviews
accessReviews.emptyState: No access reviews yet. Click New campaign to start one.
accessReviews.descriptionPlaceholder: Focus and rationale for this campaign
accessReviews.closeDialogBody: Closing executes every REVOKE and MODIFY decision against the live tenant memberships. CONFIRM decisions stay untouched. A signed PDF evidence artifact will be generated and linked to the campaign. This cannot be undone.

coverage.title: Coverage Dashboard
coverage.description: How your assets are protected by controls and what risks remain unmitigated
coverage.allAssetsCovered: All critical assets are covered by controls

schemes.title: Certification Schemes
schemes.listDescription: Certification standards your practices and records are mapped to.
schemes.noResultsDescription: Try a different term or clear the search.
schemes.emptyDescription: Create a scheme — its requirements become the checklist your practices are mapped against.
schemes.modalSubheading: Define the scheme and the requirements practices will be mapped to.
schemes.discardConfirm: Discard scheme? Anything you entered will be lost.

frameworks.title: Certification Schemes
frameworks.description: Browse standards, install control packs, and track requirement coverage
frameworks.emptyMessage: No frameworks available. Run the seed to populate.

customModal.title: Custom frameworks
customModal.subtitle: Where customisation lives today, and what's coming.
customModal.todayBody: — the canonical path is to import a catalogue framework (ISO27001, NIS2, …) and then customise per requirement on the framework's Customize tab. Reorder requirements, link controls, and add per-tenant metadata — every change is tenant-scoped.
customModal.comingSoonBody: — tenant-scoped frameworks built from scratch. The current Framework model is global (one Framework row shared across every tenant); fully bespoke frameworks need a tenantId column + matching RLS policies. Queued behind the larger framework-lifecycle epic so the migration lands with the rest of the customisation surface.

frameworks.packsEmpty: No packs available for this framework.
frameworks.builderRestricted: Reordering is restricted to OWNER / ADMIN roles.
frameworks.installIntro: This will create controls, tasks, and requirement mappings for your tenant.
frameworks.specifyFrom: Specify a ?from=FRAMEWORK_KEY query parameter to compare.
frameworks.diffExplain: This page compares the "from" framework to this framework to show added/removed/changed requirements.

client.heading: Борса / Exchange

spray.techniqueDesc: Незадължително — техниката, с която е извършено третирането.

invite.notAvailableBody: This invite link has expired, been revoked, or already been used. Ask your admin to send a new invite.
invite.mismatchBody: You are signed in as {email}, but this invite was sent to a different email address. Sign in with the correct account to accept it.

portfolio.title: Portfolio Overview
portfolio.emptyDescEdit: Run the backfill script (`npm run db:backfill-org-widgets -- --execute`) or add widgets via the picker.
portfolio.emptyDescReader: No widgets configured. Contact an org admin.

sections.emptyTenantsDesc: Add tenants to this organization to see per-tenant coverage and health.

audit.title: Audit Log
audit.description: Immutable, hash-chained record of org-level privilege changes. Append-only — entries cannot be modified or removed.
audit.emptyDesc: Member additions, removals, role changes, and provisioning fan-out will appear here as they happen.
audit.summaryProvisioned: {count, plural, one {# tenant provisioned} other {# tenants provisioned}}
audit.summaryDeprovisioned: {count, plural, one {# tenant deprovisioned} other {# tenants deprovisioned}}

members.tooltipCannotChangeOwnRole: You cannot change your own role
members.tooltipCannotRemoveSelf: You cannot remove yourself from the organization
members.title: Org Members
members.membersSummary: {count, plural, one {# member} other {# members}} across this organization. ORG_ADMINs are auto-provisioned as AUDITORs in every linked tenant; ORG_READERs see the portfolio summary only.
members.emptyDesc: Invite your first member to start managing this organization.
members.addEmailDesc: The user is created as a placeholder if they have not signed in yet.
members.roleAdminDesc: Manages tenants + members. Auto-provisioned as AUDITOR in every child tenant.
members.roleReaderDesc: Sees the portfolio summary. No tenant drill-down access.
members.roleAdminDescAccept: Manages tenants + members. Auto-provisioned as AUDITOR in every child tenant on accept.
members.removeAdminWarning: This member is an ORG_ADMIN. Their auto-provisioned AUDITOR memberships will be removed from every child tenant. Manually-granted memberships are preserved.
members.promotionCallout: Promoting to ORG_ADMIN will also create an AUDITOR membership for this user in every child tenant of the organization.
members.demotionCallout: Demoting to ORG_READER will remove the auto-provisioned AUDITOR membership from every child tenant. Manually-granted tenant memberships are preserved.
members.invitationEmailed: Invitation emailed to {email}. The link expires in 7 days and is single-use.
members.inviteCreatedNoEmail: The invite was created but the email could not be sent — share this acceptance link with {email} directly. It expires in 7 days and is single-use.
members.copyLinkAlso: You can also copy the link below to share it another way.
members.inviteTip: Tip: bookmark this — closing the dialog won't re-display the URL. The pending invite is also visible in the list below.
members.inviteEmailDesc: The recipient receives a single-use acceptance link valid for 7 days.

tenants.title: Tenant Health
tenants.tenantsSummary: {count, plural, one {# tenant} other {# tenants}} linked to this organization
tenants.emptyDesc: Add tenants to this organization to populate the portfolio view.
tenants.removeBody: This removes {name} from the organization. Its members lose access immediately and it disappears from the portfolio. The workspace data is retained (not erased), so it can be restored by an administrator.

newTenant.frameworkLaterDesc: Skip framework selection — pick on the new tenant dashboard.
newTenant.nameTooLong: Name must be 120 characters or fewer.
newTenant.slugInvalid: Slug must be lowercase letters, numbers, and dashes (no leading or trailing dashes).
newTenant.createDesc: Tenants are isolated workspaces under this organization. Other org admins are auto-provisioned with AUDITOR access on creation.
newTenant.nameDesc: Display name shown across the tenant workspace and audit reports.
newTenant.slugDesc: Lowercase URL identifier. Auto-derived from the name; edit if you need something different.
newTenant.startingFrameworkDesc: Pick the compliance framework you want to install first. You can add more (or change this choice) from the new tenant dashboard.

profile.avatarNote: Upload a square image — it is resized to 256×256 and stored as WebP. Photo metadata, including location, is stripped in your browser before upload.
profile.nameSaved: Saved — your name now shows on owner and assignee columns.
profile.nameValidation: Enter at least a first or last name.

feedback.title: Sound & haptics
feedback.description: Confirmation when you mark field work done. Stays silent on devices that don’t support it.
feedback.successSoundAria: Success sound on marking work done
feedback.hapticFeedbackAria: Haptic feedback on marking work done

changePassword.title: Change password
changePassword.successNotice: Your password has been changed. Redirecting to sign in…
changePassword.errFillAll: Please fill in all three password fields.
changePassword.errMismatch: The two new passwords do not match.
changePassword.errTooShort: Your new password must be at least 8 characters long.
changePassword.errSameAsCurrent: Your new password must be different from your current password.

forgot.title: Reset your password
forgot.submittedNotice: If an account exists for that email, we've sent a password reset link. The link expires in 1 hour.
forgot.intro: Enter the email address for your account and we'll send you a link to reset your password.

reset.title: Choose a new password
reset.invalidLink: This password reset link is invalid. Request a new one from the sign-in page.
reset.errFillAll: Please fill in both password fields.
reset.errMismatch: The two passwords do not match.
reset.errTooShort: Your new password must be at least 8 characters long.

invite.notAvailableBody: This invite link has expired, been revoked, or already been used. Ask your admin to send a new invite.
invite.mismatchBody: You are signed in as {email}, but this invite was sent to a different email address. Sign in with the correct account to accept it.

noTenant.title: No access yet
noTenant.body: You do not have access to any workspace yet. Ask your admin to invite you, then follow the link in the invitation email.

notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.body: Sorry, we couldn't find the page you're looking for. It might have been removed or the link is incorrect.

tenants.title: Choose a workspace
tenants.subtitle: You are a member of multiple workspaces. Select one to continue.

vendorAssessment.errorTitle: This link is no longer active
vendorAssessment.errorExpired: The questionnaire has expired. Please contact the requester for a new invitation.
vendorAssessment.errorWrongStatus: This questionnaire has already been submitted or closed.
vendorAssessment.errorGeneric: We could not open the questionnaire. Please double-check the link in your invitation email.
vendorAssessment.submittedBody: Thank you. Your responses have been delivered to the requester for review.
vendorAssessment.submitFailed: Submission failed. Please try again in a moment.
vendorAssessment.fileUploadNote: File-upload responses are coordinated through your contact at this stage. Please add a note here describing the file you intend to share.
vendorAssessment.fileUploadPlaceholder: e.g. Will send DPA via secure email
